pr.title: Fix gmtitle to match, mark as matching, ninja apply
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Made the gmtitle object fully matching and promoted it in configure.py from Equivalent to Matching. The source fix was a small behavior-preserving refactor in src/melee/gm/gmtitle.c: extract the title/opening-VS state test into static inline bool isActiveTitle(), change fn_801A1498_inline from static inline to static, and call the helper in the animation start-frame branch. The ninja/symbol portion localized many gmtitle literals/constants, added local section labels, and split gmtitle BSS symbols. decomp.dev reported two new data matches for main/melee/gm/gmtitle: .bss and .sdata2.
key_files: src/melee/gm/gmtitle.c — added isActiveTitle(), changed fn_801A1498_inline storage/inlining, and replaced an inline boolean branch block with a helper call.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt — ninja-applied symbol updates: local @NN labels for literals/constants, local ...data.0/...bss.0 labels, and a split of gmTitle BSS objects.
what_changed: Added static inline bool isActiveTitle() for the condition gm_801A4310() == MJ_TITLE || (gm_801A4310() == MJ_OPENING_MV && gm_801A42C4() == MN_VS), returning false for that case and true otherwise.
Changed fn_801A1498_inline from static inline void to static void, while keeping the existing function name.
Replaced the local var_r0 assignment branch in fn_801A1498_inline with if (isActiveTitle() != 0), preserving the same choice between gmTitle_803DA4FC.start_frame and 130.0F for HSD_JObjReqAnimAll.
Promoted melee/gm/gmtitle.c in configure.py from Equivalent to Matching.
Reclassified gmtitle-related strings and constants in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from generic lbl_/gmTitle_ globals to local @NN symbols with scope:local across .rodata, .data, .sdata, and .sdata2.
Added local section labels ...data.0 at .data:0x803DA4F0 and ...bss.0 at .bss:0x80479B28.
Split gmTitle_80479B28 in .bss from one global 0xA0 object into gmTitle_80479B28 size 0x10 plus local gmTitle_80479B38 size 0x10 and gmTitle_80479B48 size 0x80.
decomp-dev[bot] reported linked code +3936 bytes, matched data +192 bytes, linked data +584 bytes, and two new matches for main/melee/gm/gmtitle.
smart_moves: Used a minimal semantic-preserving helper extraction instead of rewriting title-screen behavior; this is a good way to tune MWCC inlining/control-flow output while keeping the logic readable.
Separated the matching source change from the symbol-map cleanup but committed both together, so the object could be marked Matching only after code and data layout were aligned.
Localized private literals/constants as @NN scope:local symbols instead of leaving them as misleading lbl_ or gmTitle_ globals.
Fixed BSS granularity by splitting a 0xA0 blob into exact smaller objects, which directly contributed to the .bss match going from 50.00% to 100.00% for gmtitle.
Kept the configure.py status update explicit and easy to audit: Equivalent to Matching for only melee/gm/gmtitle.c.
decomp_lessons: An Equivalent object may need only a tiny codegen-focused refactor plus symbol metadata corrections before it can be promoted to Matching.
For MWCC matching, moving a condition into a static inline helper and changing the caller from static inline to static can be enough to alter emitted code without changing behavior.
When literals or constants are compiler-local, prefer @NN symbols with scope:local in symbols.txt over address-style global names.
Data matching can depend on BSS object boundaries; do not leave one large global BSS symbol if the source actually emits multiple adjacent local/static objects.
After a ninja apply or other symbol-tool pass, review scope, size, align, and section labels together because all of them can affect linked/matched data accounting.
naming_conventions: Global title assets keep the gmTitle_ prefix, e.g. gmTitle_803DA4F0, gmTitle_803DA4FC, gmTitle_803DA508, and gmTitle_80479B28.
Local compiler-generated data labels in symbols.txt use @NNN names with scope:local, e.g. @156, @173, @239, @245 through @256, @335 through @338.
Local section labels use dotted names such as ...data.0 and ...bss.0.
The PR introduced the readable helper name isActiveTitle for the title/opening-VS predicate.
The diff evidence uses MJ_TITLE, MJ_OPENING_MV, and MN_VS enum-style names in the extracted condition.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Extracting the title-state predicate into isActiveTitle() is the main source-level matching tactic.

Changing fn_801A1498_inline from static inline to static likely targeted inlining/code-layout differences while preserving the call site behavior; the PR title and Matching promotion support that this was for match.
Replacing the explicit var_r0 true/false branch with a helper call simplified the visible C while matching the desired output.
Ninja-applied symbol changes converted many gmtitle data items to local @NN labels and added local section labels.
BSS split tactic: gmTitle_80479B28 0xA0 became 0x10 plus gmTitle_80479B38 0x10 plus gmTitle_80479B48 0x80, enabling the reported .bss 100% match.
configure.py should only be switched from Equivalent to Matching after the object and symbol map agree with the build/report.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were captured in the slice; the PR body was empty.
decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 progress: linked code 31.62% (+0.10%, +3936 bytes), matched data 34.58% (+0.02%, +192 bytes), and linked data 28.99% (+0.05%, +584 bytes).
Bot report listed 2 new matches for main/melee/gm/gmtitle: .bss +80 bytes from 50.00% to 100.00%, and .sdata2 +4 bytes from 87.50% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Search for other Object(Equivalent, "melee/gm/*.c") entries where a small inline/helper refactor plus symbol cleanup could promote to Matching.
Search config/GALE01/symbols.txt for remaining gmTitle_ or lbl_ gmtitle literals that should be local @NN symbols.
Audit fn_801A1498_inline for the now-unused bool var_r0 declaration left visible in the diff after replacing the branch with isActiveTitle().
Look for later PRs touching isActiveTitle, fn_801A1498_inline, or gmTitle_80479B28/gmTitle_80479B38/gmTitle_80479B48 to see whether this matching tactic was refined.
